ifc 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Convert to an IFC datetime

Source: R/datetime.R
ifc_datetime.Rd

Convert a POSIXct, POSIXlt, character (ISO 8601 datetime), ifc_date, or numeric (seconds since epoch) to an ifc_datetime object.

Usage

ifc_datetime(x, tz = "UTC")

Arguments

x

A datetime-like object.

tz

Time zone string (e.g. "UTC", "America/New_York"). For POSIXct input and tz is not supplied, defaults to the timezone already stored in x. For ifc_date input, the datetime is placed at midnight in tz.

Value

An ifc_datetime vector.

Examples

ifc_datetime("2024-07-14 09:30:00", tz = "America/New_York")
#> <ifc_datetime[1] tz=America/New_York>
#> [1] "2024-07-27 09:30:00 EDT"
ifc_datetime(ifc_ymd(2024, 7, 14), tz = "UTC")
#> <ifc_datetime[1] tz=UTC>
#> [1] "2024-07-14 00:00:00 UTC"
ifc_datetime(Sys.time())
#> <ifc_datetime[1] tz=UTC>
#> [1] "2026-03-18 22:49:40 UTC"
